summary: Complete verification of multi-word @mention bug fix and notification click navigation. All tests pass - 10/10 backend tests, frontend verification successful.

bug1_multiword_mentions.description: @mentioned users were NOT receiving notifications because regex @(\S+) only captured one word (e.g. '@Gerardo' from '@Gerardo Avina')
bug1_multiword_mentions.fix_details: Backend now fetches all users and checks if '@{user_name.lower()}' exists as substring in comment content
tests_passed: @Gerardo Avina creates mention notification for user_27e8c806ee2e
@Luke Hoefs creates mention notification for user_66b5d928cee7
Multiple mentions in one comment work
Comments without mentions still create generic notifications

bug2_notification_click_redirect.description: Clicking notification should redirect to order's board AND open CommentsModal
bug2_notification_click_redirect.fix_details: Notification click handler now: 1) finds order, 2) switches board, 3) opens CommentsModal with setTimeout(300ms)
tests_passed: Click notification -> board switches to order's board
Click notification -> CommentsModal opens showing order comments
@ icon displays for mention-type notifications (not comment-type)

frontend_playwright.notification_dropdown: PASS - Opens correctly with @ icon for mention notifications
frontend_playwright.notification_click: PASS - Switches board and opens CommentsModal

database_verification.gerardo_mention_notifications: Multiple 'mention' type notifications created with correct order_id
database_verification.luke_mention_notifications: Multiple 'mention' type notifications created with correct order_id

verification_evidence.gerardo_notifications_screenshot: Shows 7 unread notifications, @ icon visible for mention types
verification_evidence.comments_modal_screenshot: Shows order DIRECT-CHECK-953433 with @Gerardo mention highlighted
verification_evidence.board_switch: Confirmed board changed to 'PAPELERA DE RECICLAJE' after notification click

context_for_next_testing_agent: Multi-word @mention bug FIXED and verified. Backend now uses substring matching instead of regex. Frontend notification click handler correctly switches board and opens CommentsModal. @ icon displays for type='mention' notifications. Test files at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration22_multiword_mentions.py
